title: eulychnia breviflora subsp. iquiquensis
intro: Eulychnia breviflora subsp. iquiquensis, also known as the Iquique prickly pear cactus, is a rare and endemic species of cactus found in the Atacama Desert in Chile. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eulychnia breviflora subsp. iquiquensis include its potential to aid in the healing of skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a salve or ointment to apply topically, and it has also been used in traditional medicine to make teas and infusions to treat various health issues.
